briefdescription.para: Linear curve: the velocity is constant.
Bicubic ease-in: accelerate from zero velocity.
Bicubic ease-in: decelerate to zero velocity.
Bicubic ease-in-out: accelerate from zero velocity, decelerate to zero velocity.
Custom (user-provided) animation curve.
User-provided interpolation function.
Values that are used to indicate the different animation curves, which determine the speed at which the animated value(s) change(s).
The function pointer type of a custom animation curve.

parameterdescription.para: The linear normalized animation distance to be curved.

briefdescription.para: The type used to represent how far an animation has progressed. This is passed to the animation's update handler.
Pointer to function that (optionally) prepares the animation for running. This callback is called when the animation is added to the scheduler.

parameterdescription.para: The animation that needs to be set up.

briefdescription.para: The function pointer type of the handler that will be called when an animation is started, just before updating the first frame of the animation.

parameterdescription.para: The animation that was started.
The pointer to custom, application specific data, as set using animation_set_handlers()

simplesect.para: This is called after any optional delay as set by animation_set_delay() has expired.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* AnimationStoppedHandler) (Animation *animation, bool finished, void *context)
memberdef.name: AnimationStoppedHandler

briefdescription.para: The function pointer type of the handler that will be called when the animation is stopped.

parameterdescription.para: The animation that was stopped.
True if the animation was stopped because it was finished normally, or False if the animation was stopped prematurely, because it was unscheduled before finishing.
The pointer to custom, application specific data, as set using animation_set_handlers()

simplesect.para: This animation (i.e.: the animation parameter) may be destroyed here. It is not recommended to unschedule or destroy a different Animation within this Animation's stopped handler.

briefdescription.para: Pointer to function that (optionally) cleans up the animation. This callback is called when the animation is removed from the scheduler. In case the .setup implementation allocated any memory, this is a good place to release that memory again.

parameterdescription.para: The animation that needs to be teared down.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* AnimationUpdateImplementation) (Animation *animation, const AnimationProgress progress)
memberdef.name: AnimationUpdateImplementation

briefdescription.para: Pointer to function that updates the animation according to the given normalized progress. This callback will be called repeatedly by the animation scheduler whenever the animation needs to be updated.

parameterdescription.para: The animation that needs to update; gets passed in by the animation framework.

The current normalized progress; gets passed in by the animation framework for each animation frame. The value ANIMATION_NORMALIZED_MIN represents the start and ANIMATION_NORMALIZED_MAX represents the end. Values outside this range (generated by a custom curve function) can be used to implement features like a bounce back effect, where the progress exceeds the desired final value before returning to complete the animation. When using a system provided curve function, each frame during the animation will have a progress value between ANIMATION_NORMALIZED_MIN and ANIMATION_NORMALIZED_MAX based on the animation duration and the AnimationCurve. For example, say an animation was scheduled at t = 1.0s, has a delay of 1.0s, a duration of 2.0s and a curve of AnimationCurveLinear. Then the .update callback will get called on t = 2.0s with distance_normalized = ANIMATION_NORMALIZED_MIN. For each frame thereafter until t = 4.0s, the update callback will get called where distance_normalized is (ANIMATION_NORMALIZED_MIN
(((ANIMATION_NORMALIZED_MAX - ANIMATION_NORMALIZED_MIN) * t) / duration)). Other system animation curve functions will result in a non-linear relation between distance_normalized and time.

briefdescription.para: Creates a new Animation on the heap and initalizes it with the default values.

listitem.para:
Scheduled: no
A pointer to the animation. NULL if the animation could not be created

briefdescription.para: Destroys an Animation previously created by animation_create.

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Gets the application-specific callback context of the animation. This void pointer is passed as an argument when the animation system calls AnimationHandlers callbacks. The context pointer can be set to point to any application specific data using animation_set_handlers().

parameterdescription.para: The animation.

briefdescription.para: Gets the animation curve for the animation.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to get the curve.

simplesect.para: The type of curve.

briefdescription.para: Gets the custom animation curve function for the animation.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to get the curve.

simplesect.para: The custom animation curve function for the given animation. NULL if not set.

briefdescription.para: Get the delay of an animation in milliseconds.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to get the setting

simplesect.para: the delay in milliseconds

briefdescription.para: Get the static duration of an animation from start to end (ignoring how much has already played, if any).

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to get the duration
if true, include the delay time
if true, incorporate the play_count

simplesect.para: the duration, in milliseconds. This includes any optional delay a set using animation_set_delay.

briefdescription.para: Get the current location in the animation.

simplesect.para: The animation must be scheduled to get the elapsed time. If it is not schedule, this method will return false.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to fetch the elapsed.
pointer to variable that will contain the elapsed time in milliseconds

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Gets the implementation of the custom animation.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to get the implementation.

simplesect.para: NULL if animation implementation has not been setup.

briefdescription.para: Get the play count of an animation.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to get the setting

simplesect.para: the play count

briefdescription.para: Get the reverse setting of an animation.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to get the setting

simplesect.para: the reverse setting
True if the animation was scheduled, or false if it was not.

An animation will be scheduled when it is running and not finished yet. An animation that has finished is automatically unscheduled. For convenience, passing in a NULL animation argument will simply return false

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to get its scheduled state.

briefdescription.para: Schedules the animation. Call this once after configuring an animation to get it to start running.

detaileddescription.para: If the animation's implementation has a .setup callback it will get called before this function returns.

simplesect.para: If the animation was already scheduled, it will first unschedule it and then re-schedule it again. Note that in that case, the animation's .stopped handler, the implementation's .teardown and .setup will get called, due to the unscheduling and scheduling.

parameterdescription.para: The animation to schedule.

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Create a new sequence animation from a list of 2 or more other animations. The returned animation owns the animations that were provided as arguments and no further write operations on those handles are allowed. The variable length argument list must be terminated with a NULL ptr.

simplesect.para: the maximum number of animations that can be supplied to this method is 20

parameterdescription.para: the first required component animation
the second required component animation
either the third component, or NULL if only adding 2 components

simplesect.para: The newly created sequence animation

briefdescription.para: An alternate form of animation_sequence_create() that accepts an array of other animations.

simplesect.para: the maximum number of elements allowed in animation_array is 256

parameterdescription.para: an array of component animations to include
the number of elements in the animation_array

simplesect.para: The newly created sequence animation

briefdescription.para: Sets the animation curve for the animation.

simplesect.para: Trying to set an attribute when an animation is immutable will return false (failure). An animation is immutable once it has been added to a sequence or spawn animation or has been scheduled.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to set the curve.
The type of curve.

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Sets a custom animation curve function.

simplesect.para: Trying to set an attribute when an animation is immutable will return false (failure). An animation is immutable once it has been added to a sequence or spawn animation or has been scheduled.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to set the curve.
The custom animation curve function.

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Sets an optional delay for the animation.

simplesect.para: Trying to set an attribute when an animation is immutable will return false (failure). An animation is immutable once it has been added to a sequence or spawn animation or has been scheduled.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to set the delay.
The delay in milliseconds that the animation system should wait from the moment the animation is scheduled to starting the animation.

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Sets the time in milliseconds that an animation takes from start to finish.

simplesect.para: Trying to set an attribute when an animation is immutable will return false (failure). An animation is immutable once it has been added to a sequence or spawn animation or has been scheduled.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to set the duration.
The duration in milliseconds of the animation. This excludes any optional delay as set using animation_set_delay().

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Seek to a specific location in the animation. Only forward seeking is allowed. Returns true if successful, false if the passed in seek location is invalid.

parameterdescription.para: the animation for which to set the elapsed.
the new elapsed time in milliseconds

simplesect.para: true if successful, false if the requested elapsed is invalid.

briefdescription.para: Sets the callbacks for the animation. Often an application needs to run code at the start or at the end of an animation. Using this function is possible to register callback functions with an animation, that will get called at the start and end of the animation.

simplesect.para: Trying to set an attribute when an animation is immutable will return false (failure). An animation is immutable once it has been added to a sequence or spawn animation or has been scheduled.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to set up the callbacks.
The callbacks.
A pointer to application specific data, that will be passed as an argument by the animation subsystem when a callback is called.

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Sets the implementation of the custom animation. When implementing custom animations, use this function to specify what functions need to be called to for the setup, frame update and teardown of the animation.

simplesect.para: Trying to set an attribute when an animation is immutable will return false (failure). An animation is immutable once it has been added to a sequence or spawn animation or has been scheduled.

parameterdescription.para: The animation for which to set the implementation.
The structure with function pointers to the implementation of the setup, update and teardown functions.

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Set an animation to play N times. The default is 1.

simplesect.para: Trying to set an attribute when an animation is immutable will return false (failure). An animation is immutable once it has been added to a sequence or spawn animation or has been scheduled.

parameterdescription.para: the animation to set the play count of
number of times to play this animation. Set to ANIMATION_PLAY_COUNT_INFINITE to make an animation repeat indefinitely.

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Set an animation to run in reverse (or forward)

simplesect.para: Trying to set an attribute when an animation is immutable will return false (failure). An animation is immutable once it has been added to a sequence or spawn animation or has been scheduled.

parameterdescription.para: the animation to operate on
set to true to run in reverse, false to run forward

simplesect.para: true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Create a new spawn animation from a list of 2 or more other animations. The returned animation owns the animations that were provided as arguments and no further write operations on those handles are allowed. The variable length argument list must be terminated with a NULL ptr.

simplesect.para: the maximum number of animations that can be supplied to this method is 20

parameterdescription.para: the first required component animation
the second required component animation
either the third component, or NULL if only adding 2 components

simplesect.para: The newly created spawn animation or NULL on failure

briefdescription.para: An alternate form of animation_spawn_create() that accepts an array of other animations.

simplesect.para: the maximum number of elements allowed in animation_array is 256

parameterdescription.para: an array of component animations to include
the number of elements in the animation_array

simplesect.para: The newly created spawn animation or NULL on failure

briefdescription.para: Unschedules the animation, which in effect stops the animation.

parameterdescription.para: The animation to unschedule.

simplesect.para: If the animation was not yet finished, unscheduling it will cause its .stopped handler to get called, with the "finished" argument set to false.
If the animation is not scheduled or NULL, calling this routine is effectively a no-op
true if successful, false on failure

briefdescription.para: Unschedules all animations of the application.

Constant to indicate "infinite" duration. This can be used with animation_set_duration() to indicate that the animation should run indefinitely. This is useful when implementing for example a frame-by-frame simulation that does not have a clear ending (e.g. a game).

simplesect.para: Note that distance_normalized parameter that is passed into the .update implementation is meaningless in when an infinite duration is used.
This can be returned by animation_get_duration (if the play count is infinite)

briefdescription.para: The normalized distance at the end of the animation.
The normalized distance at the start of the animation.
Constant to indicate infinite play count. Can be passed to animation_set_play_count() to repeat indefinitely.

simplesect.para: This can be returned by animation_get_play_count().

briefdescription.para: Abstract framework to create arbitrary animations

detaileddescription.para: The Animation framework provides your Pebble app with an base layer to create arbitrary animations. The simplest way to work with animations is to use the layer frame PropertyAnimation, which enables you to move a Layer around on the screen. Using animation_set_implementation(), you can implement a custom animation.
Refer to the User Interface Layers chapter in the Pebble Developer Guides (chapter "Animation") for a conceptual overview of the animation framework and on how to write custom animations.
